sections.paragraph: (according to Simonis, “sojourning,” “lodging-place,” from the root גרר i.q. גּוּר; compare Gen 20:1 perhaps also water-pots, Arab. جِرَارُ), Gerar, pr.n. of a city, formerly the abode of the kings of the Philistines; in the time of the patriarchs, subject to king Abimelech, Gen 20:1 26:6 נַחַל גְּרָר “the valley of Gerar,” Gen 26:17.
